$schema-note: Labeled detection corpus for WS2a/WS2b (reliability-hardening-track). Each case is { text, direction?, expect: [{ type, present }], note }. `present:true` = the engine SHOULD detect this type in `text`; `present:false` = it must NOT (a hard negative). `direction` defaults to "request"; injection rules are response-direction only. Scored per-type by scripts/bench-detection.mjs; regression-gated against scripts/detection-baseline.json.
$safety: ALL VALUES ARE SYNTHETIC TEST FIXTURES. No real secret, key, or PII appears here. Card numbers are the public 4242.../4111... test cards; AWS uses the documented AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE; the SSN is the public misprinted-wallet 078-05-1120; the IBAN is the public ECB example DE89370400440532013000; the JWT/PEM/tokens are fabricated or public RFC/sample values. Storing real secrets/PII is forbidden by CLAUDE.md.
$baseline-legend: WS2b closed the credential/PII coverage gaps WS2a measured: the AWS/GitHub/Google/Slack keys, JWT, and PEM headers are now true-positives, and new international-PII types (us_ssn, iban) plus E.164/US phone forms were added — each with a hard-negative case and (for us_ssn/iban) a validator-boundary case. The baseline is re-pinned to capture the improved per-type precision/recall. The gate still fails only on a REGRESSION below baseline.

cases.text: Please contact me at minji.kim@example.com about the invoice.
cases.note: clean positive: ordinary RFC-style example email

cases.text: Reach support via support@haechi.example.co.kr for help.
cases.note: clean positive: multi-label TLD email

cases.text: The file path is config@v2/build and the at-sign is decorative here.
cases.note: clean negative: @ without a valid domain is not an email

cases.text: My mobile is 010-1234-5678, call anytime.
cases.note: clean positive: separated KR mobile

cases.text: Call +82 10 1234 5678 or the no-separator form 01012345678.
cases.note: clean positive: +82 international form and 0-led bare run

cases.text: Order id 01099887766 was shipped on time.
cases.note: FALSE POSITIVE the audit reproduced: an 11-digit order id starting 010 currently mis-fires as a KR phone. Hard-negative — the engine should NOT flag an order id as a phone. (Records as a current FP in the baseline; WS2c context anchors will close it.)

cases.text: The unix timestamp 1781129892 and counter 1719999999 are not phone numbers.
cases.note: clean negative: bare 10-digit non-0-led runs are rejected by krPhoneValid

cases.text: synthetic resident registration number 900101-1234568 on the form
cases.note: clean positive: KR RRN-shaped value with a VALID checksum (synthetic)

cases.text: synthetic rrn-shaped 900101-1234567 fails the checksum
cases.note: clean negative: RRN shape but checksum-invalid -> not detected

cases.text: card on file 4242 4242 4242 4242 expires soon
cases.note: clean positive: the public 4242 Visa test card (Luhn-valid)

cases.text: alt test card 4111 1111 1111 1111 for the gateway
cases.note: clean positive: the public 4111 Visa test card (Luhn-valid)

cases.text: please process order 4242424242424242 and ship today
cases.note: FALSE POSITIVE the audit reproduced: a Luhn-passing 16-digit order number in non-card context currently mis-fires as a card. Hard-negative — the engine should NOT flag a bare order number. (Records as a current FP in the baseline; WS2c context anchors will close it.)

cases.text: tracking number 123456789012345 is not a payment card
cases.note: clean negative: a 15-digit non-Luhn tracking run is rejected by luhnValid

cases.text: api key sk_demo_1234567890abcdef1234567890abcdef rotated last week
cases.note: clean positive: an sk_ prefixed key of real shape (synthetic)

cases.text: publishable key pk_demo_0123456789abcdef0123456789 in the snippet
cases.note: clean positive: a pk_ prefixed key (synthetic)

cases.text: sku_98 and pk_3 are short product codes, not keys
cases.note: clean negative: prefixes too short to meet the 24+ char rule

cases.text: Authorization: Bearer ZmFrZS10b2tlbi1mb3ItdGVzdGluZy0xMjM0NTY3OA in the header example
cases.note: clean positive: a real-shaped Bearer credential (synthetic base64-ish run)

cases.text: Bearer 0123456789abcdef is mentioned in this prose paragraph as an example phrase.
cases.note: FALSE POSITIVE the audit reproduced: the word 'Bearer' followed by a 16-char token inside prose mis-fires as a secret. Hard-negative. (Records as a current FP in the baseline; WS2c prose/context anchors will close it.)

cases.text: password: changeme123456 is a placeholder in the README, replace it.
cases.note: FALSE POSITIVE the audit reproduced: a 'password: changeme...' placeholder mis-fires as a secret. Hard-negative — placeholders must not be flagged. (Records as a current FP in the baseline; WS2c allowlist/placeholder anchors will close it.)

cases.text: secret = s3cr3t_value_kept_here in the config file
cases.note: clean positive: a secret=<value> assignment with a real-shaped value

cases.text: the meeting agenda lists three items and a budget of 4 dollars
cases.note: clean negative: ordinary prose with small numbers and words -> nothing

cases.text: version 1.2.0 ships with 47 findings across 5 lenses and 6 workstreams
cases.note: clean negative: version/count numbers must not trip any rule

cases.text: AWS access key AKIAIOSFODNN7EXAMPLE used in the docs example
cases.note: WS2b CLOSED: the documented AWS example access key (AKIA + 16 uppercase-alnum) is now detected by the anchored aws-access-key-id rule.

cases.text: the deploy ran AKIA1234 first then AKIANOTLONGENOUGH afterward
cases.note: HARD NEGATIVE (aws-access-key-id): AKIA followed by fewer than 16 uppercase-alnum chars must NOT match — the rule requires the exact AKIA/ASIA + 16 shape, not a bare AKIA prefix.

cases.text: aws_secret_access_key = wJalrXUtnFEMI/K7MDENG/bPxRfiCYEXAMPLEKEY noted here
cases.note: WS2b CLOSED: the documented AWS secret as an `aws_secret_access_key = ...` assignment is now caught by the expanded assignment-secret key vocabulary (aws_secret_access_key, client_secret, private_key, access_token, refresh_token, secret_key, api_secret).

cases.text: client_secret: 0123456789abcdef0123 was committed to the repo
cases.note: clean positive: the expanded assignment-secret vocabulary also matches the OAuth `client_secret: <value>` form.

cases.text: GitHub PAT ghp_0123456789abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzAB committed by mistake
cases.note: WS2b CLOSED: a GitHub ghp_ personal-access-token (fabricated chars) is now detected by the anchored gh[pousr]_ rule.

cases.text: the changelog mentions ghp_short and a ghp prefix without a token body
cases.note: HARD NEGATIVE (github-token): a bare `ghp_` prefix with a too-short body must NOT match — the rule requires >=36 base64-ish chars after the prefix.

cases.text: Google API key AIzaSyA1234567890abcdefghijklmnopqrstuv in the client config
cases.note: WS2b CLOSED: a Google AIza... API key (fabricated) is now detected by the anchored AIza + 35-char rule.

cases.text: the word AIza appears here as AIzaShort without a full 35-char body
cases.note: HARD NEGATIVE (google-api-key): an AIza prefix without exactly 35 trailing URL-safe chars must NOT match.

cases.text: Slack bot token xoxb-PLACEHOLDER-PLACEHOLDER-notARealSlackTokenForTests leaked
cases.note: WS2b CLOSED: a Slack xoxb- shaped token (synthetic low-entropy placeholder) is now detected by the anchored xox[baprs]- rule — which keys on prefix+body shape, not entropy.

cases.text: the deploy log printed xoxb-short and xoxe-unknownprefix here
cases.note: HARD NEGATIVE (slack-token): a too-short body (xoxb-short) and a non-allowlisted second char (xoxe-) must NOT match.

cases.text: JWT eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCJ9.eyJzdWIiOiIxMjM0NTY3ODkwIn0.dozjgNryP4J3jVmNHl0w5N_XgL0n3I9PlFUP0THsR8U in the auth flow
cases.note: WS2b CLOSED: a three-segment JWT (public jwt.io sample) is now detected by the eyJ + two-base64url-segments rule.

cases.text: the build id abc.def.ghi and a value foo.bar.baz are dotted but not JWTs
cases.note: HARD NEGATIVE (jwt): a dotted three-part token whose first segment does NOT start with `eyJ` (the base64 of `{"`) must NOT match — the eyJ anchor is the precision guard.

cases.text: -----BEGIN RSA PRIVATE KEY----- MIIBOgIBAAJBAKj34GkxFh... -----END RSA PRIVATE KEY-----
cases.note: WS2b CLOSED: a PEM PRIVATE KEY header (truncated/fabricated body) is now detected by the -----BEGIN ... PRIVATE KEY----- header rule.

cases.text: rotate the private key before deploy and store the secret material safely
cases.note: HARD NEGATIVE (pem-private-key): a sentence merely containing the words 'private key' with no PEM armor header must NOT match — the rule anchors on the -----BEGIN ... PRIVATE KEY----- header, not the prose.

cases.text: US SSN 078-05-1120 on the intake form
cases.note: WS2b CLOSED (international PII): the famous synthetic US SSN (078-05-1120, the public misprinted-wallet SSN) is now detected by the us-ssn rule + SSA-range validator. kr_rrn stays false — it is not mis-classified as a KR RRN.

cases.text: order number 666123456 and reference 078051120 are nine-digit ids
cases.note: HARD NEGATIVE (us-ssn): a bare 9-digit order/reference number (no separators) must NOT match — the rule requires the AAA-GG-SSSS separators, so a 9-digit id is never an SSN.

cases.text: intake form lists SSN 666-12-1234 which is a structurally invalid area
cases.note: HARD NEGATIVE (us-ssn validator): area 666 is never issued — the validator rejects it even though the AAA-GG-SSSS shape matches.

cases.text: IBAN DE89370400440532013000 from the ECB example set
cases.note: WS2b CLOSED (international PII): the public German IBAN example is now detected by the iban rule + mod-97 checksum validator. card stays false — the embedded 20-digit run is out of card length and not Luhn-valid.

cases.text: reference DE89370400440532013001 looks IBAN-shaped but fails mod-97
cases.note: HARD NEGATIVE (iban validator): an IBAN-shaped string whose final digit breaks the mod-97 checksum must NOT match — the checksum is what makes the rule high-precision.

cases.text: call the US line +14155552671 to confirm the booking
cases.note: WS2b CLOSED (E.164): a +1 E.164 number (leading + required) is now detected by the e164-phone rule.

cases.text: US phone (415) 555-2671 from the reserved 555-xxxx test range
cases.note: WS2b CLOSED (US national): a separated US/NANP number (415) 555-2671 is now detected by the us-phone rule.

cases.text: US phone (212) 555-0143 from the reserved 555-01xx test range
cases.note: WS2b CLOSED (US national): the previously-uncovered NANP test number is now detected by the us-phone rule (parenthesized form).

cases.text: the account id 12345678901 and ticket 4155552671 are bare digit runs
cases.note: HARD NEGATIVE (e164/us-phone): a separator-less, plus-less digit run must NOT match — E.164 requires a leading +, US national requires separators, and the kr-phone rule already guards bare runs. An id/timestamp is never a phone.
